questions.text: According to WHO, reproductive health means total well-being in which aspects?
questions.explanation: WHO defines it as a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being in all matters relating to the reproductive system.

questions.text: India was the first country in the world to initiate ________ in 1951.
questions.explanation: These programmes are now called Reproductive and Child Health Care (RCH) programmes.

questions.text: The technique used to determine the sex of the foetus and detect genetic disorders is:
questions.explanation: It involves analyzing the amniotic fluid surrounding the foetus. It is banned for sex determination in India.

questions.text: What is the statutory marriageable age for females and males in India?
questions.explanation: 18 years for females and 21 years for males to prevent early marriage and population explosion.

questions.text: An ideal contraceptive should be:
questions.explanation: It should also have least side effects and not interfere with sexual desire.

questions.text: Periodic abstinence is a natural method where couples avoid coitus from day ________ of the menstrual cycle.
questions.explanation: This is the fertile period when ovulation is most likely to occur.

questions.text: Lactational amenorrhea refers to the absence of menstruation during:
questions.explanation: High prolactin levels inhibit ovulation. It is effective for up to six months post-delivery.

questions.text: Condoms are barriers that also provide protection against:
questions.explanation: Unlike most other methods, barriers prevent the exchange of body fluids.

questions.text: IUD stands for:
questions.explanation: These are devices inserted by doctors in the uterus through vagina.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-medicated IUD?
questions.explanation: Lippes loop acts as a physical barrier to sperm migration and implantation.

questions.text: The primary function of copper-releasing IUDs (like CuT) is to:
questions.explanation: Copper ions released suppress the fertilising capacity of sperms.

questions.text: Oral contraceptive 'Saheli' was developed by scientists at:
questions.explanation: Central Drug Research Institute (CDRI) Lucknow developed this 'once-a-week' non-steroidal pill.

questions.text: The surgical method of sterilization in males is called:
questions.explanation: A small part of the vas deferens is removed or tied up.

questions.text: The surgical method of sterilization in females is called:
questions.explanation: A small part of the fallopian tube is removed or tied up.

questions.text: Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP) is considered safe up to how many weeks?
questions.explanation: MTP is safe during the first trimester (up to 12 weeks).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a sexually transmitted infection?
questions.explanation: Asthma is a respiratory disorder, not an STI.

questions.text: Hepatitis-B and HIV can also be transmitted by:
questions.explanation: These infections spread through contact with infected blood and fluids.

questions.text: Infertility is the inability to produce children even after ________ of unprotected sexual cohabitation.
questions.explanation: Standard clinical definition of infertility.

questions.text: In 'Test Tube Baby' programme, the embryo is transferred into the woman's body. This is called:
questions.explanation: ET includes techniques like ZIFT and IUT.

questions.text: ZIFT stands for:
questions.explanation: The zygote or early embryo (up to 8 blastomeres) is transferred into the fallopian tube.

questions.text: GIFT involves the transfer of ________ into the fallopian tube of a female who cannot produce them.
questions.explanation: Gamete Intra Fallopian Transfer is used when the female can provide environment for fertilization but cannot produce eggs.

questions.text: ICSI is a specialized procedure where a sperm is:
questions.explanation: Intra Cytoplasmic Sperm Injection is an ART technique used in labs.

questions.text: In Artificial Insemination (AI), the semen is introduced into the:
questions.explanation: Used when the male partner has low sperm count.

questions.text: Which STI is completely curable if detected early and treated properly?
questions.explanation: Except for HIV, Hepatitis-B, and Genital herpes, most STIs are curable.

questions.text: The pill 'Saheli' is a ________ pill with ________ side effects.
questions.explanation: It is a non-steroidal oral contraceptive.

questions.text: Which hormone-releasing IUD makes the uterus unsuitable for implantation?
questions.explanation: Progestasert and LNG-20 are hormone-releasing IUDs.

questions.text: World population which was 2 billion in 1900 rocketed to about ________ by 2011.
questions.explanation: Significant increase due to better health facilities and lower death rates.

questions.text: MTP is mainly used to:
questions.explanation: Legalized in India in 1971 with strict conditions to avoid misuse.

questions.text: Sterilization is also known as ________ method.
questions.explanation: It is a permanent method to prevent any more pregnancies.

questions.text: Emergency contraceptives are effective if used within ________ hours of coitus.
questions.explanation: Progestogens or IUDs can be used as emergency methods.

questions.text: The oral contraceptive pill 'Saheli' is unique because it is:
questions.explanation: It has high contraceptive value with very few side effects due to its non-steroidal nature.

questions.text: To prevent early marriage and population explosion, the statutory marriageable age in India is:
questions.explanation: Laws set the minimum age at 18 years for females and 21 years for males.

questions.text: MTP (Medical Termination of Pregnancy) was legalized in India in which year?
questions.explanation: It was legalized with strict conditions to avoid its misuse for female foeticide.

questions.text: MTP is considered safe only up to the first ________ of pregnancy.
questions.explanation: The first trimester (up to 12 weeks) is the safest period for MTP.

questions.text: In ZIFT, the zygote is transferred into the:
questions.explanation: Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer involves placing the early embryo into the fallopian tube.

questions.text: ICSI (Intra Cytoplasmic Sperm Injection) is used when:
questions.explanation: A single sperm is directly injected into the ovum in the laboratory.

questions.text: Amniocentesis is a medical procedure used to:
questions.explanation: Legally it is for genetic testing, but it is often misused for sex determination.

questions.text: Which natural method of contraception relies on the absence of menstruation during breastfeeding?
questions.explanation: Intense lactation after delivery prevents ovulation naturally for up to six months.

questions.text: GIFT involves the transfer of ________ into the fallopian tube.
questions.explanation: Used for females who cannot produce ova but can provide a suitable environment for fertilization.

questions.text: Providing sex education in schools is important to:
options: Provide right information about reproductive life
questions.explanation: It helps young people understand physical changes and safe reproductive practices.
